subject: noise2map error
body:
Hi Dimitry,
I had emailed this problem to the group but it seems it hasnt landed here 
(or atleast I cannot see it). Therefore, I am posting this directly into 
the group.Apologies if it is repeated. The premise of question is that, I 
am trying to denoise my tomograms (for shiggles) and I am running into an 
error after running the following command:

 Noise2Map.exe --observation1 even --observation2 odd --angpix 10 
--batchsize 4 --dont_flatten_spectrum --iterations 10000 --gpuid_preprocess 
0



Loading model, 23351 MB free.
2020-10-26 17:25:35.301274: I 
D:\Dev\tensorflow\tensorflow\core\platform\cpu_feature_guard.cc:141] Your 
CPU supports instructions that this TensorFlow binary was not compiled to 
use: AVX2

Found device 0 with properties:
name: Quadro RTX 6000 major: 7 minor: 5 memoryClockRate(GHz): 1.77
pciBusID: 0000:d9:00.0
totalMemory: 24.00GiB freeMemory: 22.80GiB

with 22117 MB memory) -> physical GPU (device: 0, name: Quadro RTX 6000,

cuda_malloc

SavedModel from: C:\Users\warp\AppData\Roaming\Warp\noisenet3dmodel_64

succeeded)
   at Noise2Map.Program.ClearCurrentConsoleLine() in

Could you help me understand the first cause of death?

best,
Pranav
